message: More Israelis left than arrived this past year, according to new CBS data ahead of Rosh Hashanah. Around 79,000 emigrated, while just 25,000 immigrated. Another 5,000 entered via family reunification, and 21,000 returned from abroad. Despite this negative migration balance, Israel’s population grew to 10.148 million.

The IDF opened a temporary corridor along Salah al-Din Street to accelerate civilian evacuation from Gaza City, operating Sept. 17–19. Residents were urged to use only the designated route and follow security instructions.

The IDF launched Operation Gideon’s Chariots II in Gaza City, targeting terrorists and military infrastructure. On Monday, a Hamas weapons facility was struck, triggering secondary explosions from stored explosives. In the past two days, the IAF and artillery hit over 150 terror targets in support of ground forces.
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.
🌎 Join ambassadors, diplomats, ministers, business leaders, and global influencers at Israel’s premier policy forum. LIVE NOW!
DEVELOPING: IDF tanks began entering Gaza City late on Monday night, local Palestinian reports said, as the Israeli military ramped up its invasion of the key stronghold in the northern Strip.

Three synagogues in Halifax were vandalized overnight with swastikas and graffiti reading “Jews did 9/11,” local Jewish organizations reported. One Hebrew school group arrived Sunday morning to find the hate messages sprayed across Shaar Shalom’s facade.
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio adjusted his travel schedule — after Israel, he’ll stop in Qatar before heading to the UK, according to the State Department.

Police raided Minister May Golan’s Jerusalem office over suspected fraud, misuse of public funds, and fictitious jobs. A lawyer linked to her was arrested, and a drug lab was discovered in an advisor’s office. Golan is set to face questioning under caution.

The “Tinder Swindler,” Simon Leviev, was arrested Sunday morning upon landing in Batumi, Georgia. Authorities have not disclosed the grounds for his detention, and even his lawyers remain in the dark. Leviev, whose scams were detailed in a Netflix documentary, is infamous for posing as the heir to a diamond fortune while defrauding women he met on Tinder.

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited the Western Wall on Sunday as part of their diplomatic meetings.

📸 Marc Israel Sellem
The IDF struck another high-rise in Gaza City used by Hamas for intelligence and observation posts. Evacuation warnings were issued earlier for residents in Gaza Port and al-Rimal.
A 70-year-old man drowned in the Dead Sea near Ein Bokek. Paramedics attempted resuscitation but were forced to declare him dead at the scene.

The Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla has been stalled in Tunisian ports for a week. Activists say two vessels were damaged by drones, while others wait to join from Italy. The ships have moved between marinas in Sidi Bou Said and Bizerte without continuing toward Gaza.

A police officer injured Walla photographer Reuven Castro during a protest at a Likud event in Petah Tikva. Castro said he was pushed to the ground while covering the event and struck by an officer after the fall.
IDF intercepts missile from Yemen, causing sirens to sound in Tel Aviv, central Israel
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.
The UN General Assembly has approved the "New York Declaration on the Peaceful Settlement of the Question of Palestine," with 142 countries in favor, 10 against, and 12 abstaining.
'I think we have him': Suspect in Kirk killing taken into custody, Trump says

A 60-year-old was critically injured, while another, a 23-year-old, was moderately injured in a suspected terror attack in a hotel near Jerusalem. MDA personnel are administering first aid to both at the scene.

An Israeli official told the ‘Post’ that concerns are growing no senior Hamas leaders were killed in the IDF’s Doha strike. Bodies found at the site, initially reported as “unidentified,” are now believed not to be senior officials.

Ryanair CEO Michael O’Leary warned the airline may not return to Israel even after the Gaza war violence subsides, citing frustration with the Israel Airports Authority and urging Jerusalem to “get its act together.”

Syria’s transitional government said it captured a Hezbollah cell operating near Israel, in the towns of Sasa and Kanaker, west of Damascus. Forces seized missile launchers, 19 Grad missiles, anti-tank weapons, and large quantities of ammunition. The area lies just a few miles from the Israeli-controlled Golan ceasefire line.

Two IDF soldiers were lightly wounded when their armored personnel carrier hit an IED near the Nitzani Oz crossing by Tulkarm on Thursday. Palestinian Islamic Jihad claimed responsibility. The IDF has placed the city under lockdown with roadblocks and inspections.
The IDF said its 36th Division has completed months of operations in Khan Yunis, dismantling dozens of kilometers of Hamas tunnels and destroying underground weapons sites.

Elizabeth Tsurkov, the Israeli-Russian researcher held hostage in Iraq for 903 days, has landed in Israel and was taken to Sheba Medical Center. The Prime Minister’s Office and Mossad confirmed her return and thanked Cyprus for its role in her transfer.

Hamas said it will continue hostage deal negotiations despite Israel’s deadly strike in Doha that killed at least five. The group’s political bureau agreed to press ahead, demanding a ceasefire, Israeli withdrawal from Gaza, and guarantees on future governance.

Conservative commentator Charlie Kirk was fatally shot on Wednesday while giving a presentation at Utah Valley University, NBC News reported.

🎥 Copyright 27a
Israel strikes Houthi gov't complexes in capital Sanaa.

A 23-year-old yeshiva student was arrested at Ben-Gurion Airport on his way to Uman for draft evasion. He was transferred to a military prison after Attorney-General Gali Baharav-Miara ordered enforcement of conscription obligations.

The IDF dropped leaflets, sent texts, and broadcast recorded warnings telling Palestinians to leave Gaza City ahead of a looming ground invasion. Special Unit 504, known as “the IDF’s Mossad,” is leading the operation due to its expertise in Arabic and Palestinian culture.
IDF struck a high-rise in Gaza City used by Hamas for intelligence and observation. The military said precautions were taken to minimize civilian harm, accusing Hamas of using civilians as cover.

Israeli officials said the strike in Qatar that killed senior Hamas members could actually push hostage deal talks forward. While Hamas is expected to initially halt negotiations, officials believe talks will eventually resume. According to reports, Khaled Mashaal, who was not present at the meeting, is the only senior Hamas figure known to have survived.

Elizabeth Tsurkov, an Israeli-Russian citizen and Princeton student kidnapped in Baghdad in 2023, has been released after 900+ days, US President Trump confirmed. She was held by Kata’ib Hezbollah, an Iran-backed militia.

Hamas announced that five of its members were killed in an Israeli strike in Doha, including Humam al-Hayya, the son of senior Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya. Others named include Jihad Labad, director of Hayya’s office, and three additional Hamas operatives.
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel, Jerusalem area.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

Qatar has suspended its mediation in the Gaza war after Israel struck Hamas leaders on its soil. Doha condemned what it called “reckless Israeli behavior,” while the Hostages Forum warned families fear the hostages could now pay the price.

Israel confirmed striking Hamas leaders in Doha on Tuesday. The IDF and Shin Bet said the targets, including senior figure Khalil al-Hayya, were responsible for the October 7 massacre and years of Hamas operations.

📸: Copyright 27a

Several blasts were heard in Doha on Tuesday, with smoke rising over the Katara District, according to eyewitnesses.

📸: Copyright 27a
IDF issues largest evacuation order yet to Gaza City as military prepares for full-scale invasion.

The IDF named the fourth soldier killed in a northern Gaza tank explosion: Lt. Matan Abramovitz (21) from Ganei Tikva. He fell alongside Staff-Sgt. Uri Lamed (20), Sgt. Gadi Cotal (20), and Sgt. Amit Arye Regev.

Four IDF soldiers were killed in northern Gaza after terrorists attacked a tank near Sheikh Radwan on Monday morning. The fallen soldiers were identified as Staff-Sgt. Uri Lamed, Sgt. Gadi Cotal, and Sgt. Amit Arye Regev.

A taxi driver was caught on camera saving his elderly passenger during Monday’s terror shooting in Jerusalem. Footage shows him rushing her to safety as bullets flew overhead.

🎥 Copyright 27a/X

At least six people were killed and 21 wounded in a terror shooting at Ramot Junction in Jerusalem on Monday. Three victims are in serious condition, while seven were moderately wounded, MDA confirmed.

Credit: Copyright 27a

At least 15 people were wounded in a suspected terror shooting at Ramot Junction in Jerusalem on Monday, MDA said.

Credit: Copyright 27a
Israel Police said Adv.-St.-Sgt. Maj. Niv Ben-Mordechai Peretz, 28, from Afula, was killed after being shot during a chase after suspects on an ATV in Zalafa, northern West Bank.

A red moon will light up Israel’s skies tonight. A total lunar eclipse began at 6:28 p.m. and will last until 11:55 p.m., turning the moon a striking red.

📸 Marc Israel Sellem

Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania’s The Voice of Hind Rajab won the Silver Lion Grand Jury Prize at the Venice Film Festival. The film follows Red Crescent operators trying to comfort a Gazan girl trapped in a car after her family was killed.

A drone launched from Yemen struck Ramon Airport near Eilat on Sunday, injuring one person with shrapnel. The IDF confirmed the strike, closed the airspace, and suspended all flights after the attack. Earlier, the IAF intercepted three other drones.

Credit: Copyright 27a

A Lisbon funicular derailed after a cable failure, killing 16 and injuring 22 when it sped down a steep hill and crashed into a building, investigators said. A preliminary report found problems with the cable likely caused the deadly accident.

The family of Gaza hostage Alon Ohel believes he has lost vision in his right eye, citing frequent blinking and difficulty focusing in the Hamas video released this week. Specialists in Israel and abroad reviewed the footage, the Hostages and Missing Families Forum said.
Mahmoud Afana, a Hamas Nukhba terrorist who bragged about murdering 10 Israelis on October 7, was killed in an Israeli strike in Deir al-Balah, according to Palestinian reports.

Hamas released a video Friday showing Gaza hostages Guy Gilboa-Dalal and Alon Ohel. Gilboa-Dalal said he couldn’t believe he was still alive after 22 months of war and confirmed he is being held in Gaza City.
Italian fashion designer Giorgio Armani has died at the age of 91.

The IDF released photos showing Hamas Rafah Brigade commander Muhammad Shabana dining underground with lavish meals during Ramadan, while Gazans struggled with food shortages. The images were found at the site of the May 13 strike that killed him and senior Hamas figure Mohammed Sinwar.
A car accidentally drove into a group of people in Berlin, injuring several. Emergency services said some children suffered light injuries, while an accompanying adult was severely hurt.

A Gazan school principal publicly denounced Hamas, accusing it of betraying Islam and abandoning residents. Saad Al-Mashalal, whose son was killed by Hamas, said: “I was happy to see the hostages leave… That is the Islam I know. What Islam are those murderers a part of?”
Netanyahu removed the West Bank sovereignty issue from Thursday’s agenda after the UAE called it a “red line.” The meeting will instead focus on security concerns ahead of the UN General Assembly.

Hamas repeated claims of readiness for a full hostage-prisoner deal. Defense Minister Israel Katz dismissed the statement as “empty words,” warning Hamas must accept Israel’s terms or face intensified IDF action.
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel, Jerusalem area.

Greta Thunberg’s Global Sumud Flotilla said drones stalked its ships after storm delays. Organizers claim surveillance began 90 nautical miles from Menorca as the group resumed its attempt to break Israel’s Gaza blockade.

Hamas is blocking civilians from evacuating Gaza City ahead of an expected IDF invasion. Despite two weeks of Israeli efforts, only 60,000–80,000 out of one million residents have fled south, with most staying due to threats and attacks by Hamas terrorists.

Jerusalem protests for the release of 48 hostages escalated Wednesday with fires near the prime minister’s residence, barricades on rooftops, and demonstrations outside key government sites. Police reported burning dumpsters and tires in Rehavia and Givat Ram, causing damage to vehicles.

📸: Marc Israel Sellem
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

IDF and Shin Bet killed Musbah Salim Musbah Dayyah, head of the Mujahideen terror group that held the Bibas family hostage. Dayyah, the fourth leader of the group eliminated in Gaza, was behind terror operations in both Gaza and the West Bank.

IDF and Shin Bet killed Hamas terrorist Hazem Awni Naeem, who held hostages Emily Damari, Romi Gonen, and Naama Levy. Naeem, a senior figure in Hamas’s Gaza City brigade and close to commander Ezz al-Din Haddad, was killed in a strike in Gaza City on August 28.
Gazans interviewed ahead of the IDF’s expected takeover of Gaza City voice frustration with Hamas and despair about the future. Many say they want to leave the enclave if crossings open.
The Gaza-bound Sumud flotilla has set sail from Barcelona but is struggling with rough winds, lost boats, and slow progress — just 45 nautical miles covered in the first night.

Police arrested a kindergarten teacher and another suspect for drug trafficking, seizing nearly half a kilo of cocaine and a large sum of money. The two are suspected of distributing drugs across the country.

IDF reportedly killed dozens of terrorists in Gaza, including senior Hamas commanders Ahmad Abu Deif and Taleb Abu Atiwi. Airstrikes also destroyed military structures used by Hamas and Islamic Jihad.

A Belgian doctor is under scrutiny after writing “Jewish (Israeli)” in the medical issues section of a nine-year-old patient’s hospital report. The case came to light after a censored version of the letter circulated on social media.

Hundreds gathered in Tel Aviv to mourn Idan Shtivi, a slain hostage whose remains were recently retrieved from Gaza. His mother said: “On my birthday, he was recovered and returned to me — I’m sorry I didn’t take care of you.”
An IDF soldier was found lifeless in northern Israel. Military Police opened an investigation, with findings to be forwarded to the military attorney’s office.
A global genocide scholars’ association voted that Israel’s actions in Gaza meet the legal definition of genocide under the UN convention, as 86% of members backed the resolution.

High school students at the Gymnasia in Herzliya form the sign for the release of the hostages, and protest on the first day of the academic year. 

📸: Rei Ash

A 27-year-old Jerusalem man was arrested for spraying “There is a Holocaust in Gaza” on the Light Rail station. He had been detained twice before for the same graffiti at the Western Wall and the Church of the Holy Sepulchre.
A Holocaust memorial in Lyon was vandalized with the words “Free Gaza.” The city’s mayor condemned the act as “intolerable” and vowed that the perpetrators will be prosecuted.
Sgt. First Class (res.) Ariel Lubliner, 34, was killed in southern Gaza. The IDF said he was likely killed by friendly fire, an investigation has launched.
Hamas spokesman Abu Obeida was reportedly killed in an IDF strike in Gaza City, according to Al Arabiya. His death has been confirmed by the IDF.
The second hostage recovered in Gaza has been identified as Idan Shtivi. His remains were brought back along with those of Ilan Weiss in a joint IDF and Shin Bet operation.

The IDF announced it killed Muhammad Abd al-Aziz Abu Zubaida, the top ISIS commander in Gaza. He oversaw the group’s activities across Gaza, West Bank, and the Sinai, including funding and arming operations.
The US has denied visas to PA President Mahmoud Abbas and Palestinian officials, blocking them from attending the upcoming UN General Assembly in New York.
The IDF has recovered the body of murdered hostage Ilan Weiss in Gaza, along with the remains of another hostage whose identity has not yet been released.
Houthi PM Ghalib al-Rahawi and senior defense officials were reportedly killed in an Israeli strike on Sanaa during a speech by Abdul-Malik al-Houthi.

Families mark one year since Hamas murdered the “beautiful six” hostages in Rafah — Hersh Goldberg-Polin, Carmel Gat, Alexander Lobanov, Almog Sarusi, Ori Danino, and Eden Yerushalmi. Loved ones recall their lives, grief, and the call: “Stop the war. Bring them all home.”

ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan faces new sexual misconduct claim from a former intern, The Guardian reports. A UN watchdog is probing multiple allegations; Khan denies wrongdoing and has stepped aside pending review.
The IDF strikes dozens of terror targets across Gaza as preparations for a push into Gaza City intensify. Troops advance in Khan Yunis, demolish tunnels in Jabalia, and destroy Hamas command posts.

UN nuclear inspectors are back in Iran for the first time since cooperation was suspended. No deal yet on resuming full inspections, but the IAEA team will oversee fuel changes at the Bushehr nuclear plant.

IDF Arabic Spokesperson Avichay Adraee said the evacuation of Gaza City is “inevitable.” He added that families moving south will receive humanitarian aid, including tents, water, and distribution centers.
IDF Givati Brigade troops killed a terrorist cell and uncovered a weapons storage site on the outskirts of Gaza City, as operations expand deeper into the area.
IDF said 6 of about 20 Palestinians killed in Monday’s tank shelling at Nasser Hospital were Hamas terrorists. The army stresses the probe continues into why tank fire was approved for the operation.
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel, Jerusalem area.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.
Thousands rally in Tel Aviv as Netanyahu’s cabinet meets, demanding a hostage deal and end to the war.

An anti-Israel group is urging the Venice Film Festival to disinvite Israeli actress Gal Gadot from the premiere of In the Hand of Dante. The group also demanded the festival drop Gerard Butler over his ties to a pro-Israel organization.

🕯️ Israelis gathered at the Western Wall Monday night for Selichot prayers dedicated to the 50 hostages still held in Gaza. Captivity survivors and families participated, with former hostage Keith Siegel urging US President Trump to push for their release.

Credit: Marc Israel Sellem

Laf Sha’ariya is blocked by supporters of the families of the kidnapped, who are depicting the hostages blindfolded with yellow cloth. 

🎥 Tikva Mualem

Protesters blocked the Ayalon Highway in Tel Aviv and major roads across Israel Tuesday morning, demanding the government reach a deal to free the remaining 50 hostages. Families of the hostages urged Israelis to join, with signs reading “We’ll stop when everyone returns” and bonfires lit in the streets.

🎥 Simon

The family of hostage Nimrod Cohen released new footage Monday evening showing his abduction by Hamas into Gaza on Oct. 7. The video, recently found by the IDF, was revealed at a press conference at the Hostages’ Families Forum in Tel Aviv.

The family of hostage Nimrod Cohen will release new footage of his abduction by Hamas, recently uncovered by the IDF. A press conference is set for 6 p.m. Monday in Tel Aviv, where an image from the video will be shown.

The IDF admitted Monday it mistakenly struck Nasser Hospital in Khan Yunis, killing journalists — including a Reuters contractor — and other civilians. The military said the strike had been approved against a Hamas target but acknowledged the outcome as a serious error.
Hamas-linked officials in Gaza said a Reuters photographer and seven others were killed in an alleged Israeli strike on Nasser Hospital in Khan Yunis. Reuters reported the claims on Monday.

The IDF released graphic videos showing Hamas operatives torturing Gazans. COGAT said the footage illustrates Hamas’s brutality—oppressing civilians, using unrestrained violence, and ruling Gaza through fear.
The IDF released footage showing Israeli Air Force preparations and fighter jets taking off ahead of the strike in Yemen.

Israel launched airstrikes in Yemen’s capital Sanaa, hitting areas near the presidential complex and missile bases, residents told Reuters. Additional strikes were reported in Hodeidah.

Credit: Copyright 27a/X

A wildfire broke out Sunday in the Arazim Valley near Jerusalem, close to Mevaseret Zion. The blaze damaged a power line supplying the capital, causing traffic light malfunctions, and threatened the possible closure of Highway 1. Eight firefighting aircraft were deployed alongside ground forces.

Credit: Spokesperson for the Civil Defense, Jerusalem District

An IDF Air Force investigation revealed that the Houthi missile fired from Yemen at Israel on Friday contained cluster munitions. Shrapnel from the strike landed in central Israel, with fragments reported in a home’s backyard in Ginaton.

Ex-IDF intel officer warned Iran is preparing for a “second round,” with Hezbollah bracing for war. Reports said operatives were told to ditch their phones, as Tehran seeks revenge after recent blows. Syria, meanwhile, is pushing for a return to the 1974 deal with Israel.

Thousands joined pro-Palestinian rallies across Australia on Sunday, with organizers claiming 350,000 people took part nationwide. Demonstrations were held in Sydney, Brisbane, and Melbourne, demanding sanctions on Israel.

IDF’s 401st Brigade has resumed combat in Jabaliya, northern Gaza, dismantling Hamas infrastructure, killing terrorists, and securing operational control. Troops uncovered a tunnel and destroyed a Hamas military structure used for attacks.
Lt. Ori Gerlic killed in operational explosive accident in southern Gaza Strip.
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

Israeli forces shot a suspected terrorist at the Tamar checkpoint in Hebron on Thursday after he ignored calls to stop. The suspect, carrying an air gun, is in serious condition and will be evacuated to Shaare Zedek Medical Center.

An Arab Israeli citizen held in Lebanon for about a year has been released and returned to Israel. Saleh Abu-Hussein was transferred via Rosh Hanikra with Red Cross assistance and will reunite with his family after medical checks.

IDF troops carried out a raid on old Syrian regime outposts near Mount Hermon, uncovering over 300 weapons smuggled from Syria to Lebanon. Several suspects were detained and questioned. Forces remain active in the area to secure Israel’s northern border.

One person was shot and wounded Thursday morning in a suspected terror attack northeast of Ramallah. The victim was among a group of shepherds targeted by a masked gunman who opened fire before fleeing.

The IDF has begun its invasion of Gaza City. IDF Spokesman Brig.-Gen. Effie Defrin confirmed Wednesday: “Our forces already control the outskirts of the city.” Meanwhile, Netanyahu has ordered an acceleration of operations to capture Hamas’s remaining strongholds in Gaza.
Exclusive: IDF has released footage from the Kfir Brigade’s battle in Khan Younis.

Iran’s Defense Minister Brig.-Gen. Aziz Nasirzadeh warned that a new generation of “improved missiles” will be used against Israel in any future conflict. He claimed the weapons have significantly enhanced capabilities and are already deployed.

IDF soldiers thwarted an attempted ambush on a military post in Khan Yunis by at least 14 Hamas terrorists who emerged from tunnel shafts. Three soldiers were wounded — one seriously and two lightly — before the attack was repelled.

Defense Minister Israel Katz has approved the IDF’s plan to take over Gaza City, naming the operation “Gideon’s Chariots II.” The plan was presented to him by IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir and senior military command.

🕯️ Jerusalem held a memorial ceremony for Hersh Goldberg-Polin and the “beautiful six” hostages — Eden Yerushalmi, Carmel Gat, Almog Sarusi, Alexander Lobanov, and Ori Danino — marking one year since their murder by Hamas after 328 days in captivity.

📸: Chen G. Schimmel
IDF, Shin Bet killed terrorist responsible for kidnapping Yarden Bibas from Kibbutz Nir Oz on Oct. 7.

Train traffic through Tel Aviv has been suspended until Aug. 26 due to infrastructure damage and safety work. Hashalom Station will also remain closed for six days. Free shuttles and expanded light rail service will help ease disruptions.

Clashes outside the Knesset: combat veterans demanding government support for trauma care were met with police force. Protest leader Meir Kadush was slammed to the ground, punched, and hospitalized with a head injury.

Hamas agreed to the latest Gaza hostage-ceasefire proposal, which Qatar says is “almost identical” to a plan earlier presented by US envoy Steve Witkoff. Israel confirmed receiving the proposal but stressed its position remains unchanged – all hostages must be released as part of any deal.

Jordan will reinstate military conscription starting February 2026, following a directive from Crown Prince Hussain. The first draft will include 6,000 men turning 18 by January 2026, with monthly pay set at 100 dinars ($141).

Hamas has agreed to a new hostage-ceasefire proposal with Egyptian and Qatari mediators, a source told the ‘Post’. The deal would release 10 Israeli hostages in exchange for a 60-day ceasefire and 150 prisoners serving life sentences.

An anti-Israel activist was arrested in Amsterdam for vandalizing a WWII memorial with the words “Never again is now” ahead of a protest. He was later released, with his case referred to prosecutors. Freedom Party leader Geert Wilders condemned the act, calling protesters “pro-Hamas thugs.”

Israeli security forces foiled a planned terror attack near Ramallah, arresting two suspects in a joint IDF, Police, and Shin Bet operation. Both men had previously served prison time for security offenses and were allegedly armed.

IDF Chief of Staff Lt.-Gen. Eyal Zamir has approved a plan to send nearly 100,000 troops to encircle and conquer Gaza City. The large-scale operation aims to dismantle Hamas’ core infrastructure and symbols of control, with ground and air forces set to move in within days.

More than 400,000 people gathering right now at Hosatges Square in Tel Aviv. 

📸: Yair Palti

In a new video, hostage Matan Zangauker, 25, pleads for help after months in Hamas captivity. He describes abuse, illness, and despair, saying: “Every day I die a little more… You are letting us die.” His mother, Einav, released the footage Sunday, urging action for his return.

Former hostage Ilana Gritzwesky held a symbolic wedding for her boyfriend Matan, still held by Hamas, during a demonstration at Hostages Square in Tel Aviv on Sunday. “Our world was changed, and you were taken from me,” she said, pledging to fight for his return along with all hostages.

While missile alert sirens sounded, protesters were forced to take cover.

📸: Uriel Even Sapir
🚨 Sirens sound across central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

Gal Gadot at Hostages Square: “Your pain is our pain.” The actress visited Tel Aviv on the nationwide day of strikes and protests, meeting families of the 50 remaining hostages. Gadot spoke with Lishay Miran-Lavi, whose husband Omri was kidnapped from Kibbutz Nahal Oz on Oct. 7.

Credit: The Hostages and Missing Families Forum

Multiple people were injured in a mass shooting at a Brooklyn lounge overnight, reports said. The NYPD has not confirmed the number of wounded, though some reports suggested seven people were hospitalized after the 3:30 a.m. attack in Crown Heights.

Eleven protesters were arrested in Tel Aviv as nationwide demonstrations for the hostages escalated on Sunday. Scuffles broke out, and at Ra’anana Junction a truck driver assaulted a protester, leaving him bleeding. Thousands took to the streets with yellow ribbons, demanding the release of the 50 remaining hostages.

Nationwide protests for a hostage deal erupted across Israel, blocking major highways including Route 1 and Ra’anana Junction. Thousands of police were deployed to maintain order as families and supporters demand action from the government.

🎥 Niv Gerber/Meshanim Kivun/Kibbutz Be'eri
The IDF confirmed it struck Houthi energy infrastructure in Yemen, including the Haziz power station in Sanaa, in response to repeated missile and drone attacks against Israel.

The UN has officially blacklisted Hamas for systemic sexual crimes committed on Oct. 7 — a move the Foreign Ministry calls bold and declarative. The UN also pressed Israel for full access to investigate alleged detainee violations.
Mossad chief Barnea met Qatar’s PM in Doha. A senior source said he clarified a partial hostage deal is no longer being considered, despite Hamas signaling willingness to resume talks.
TIFF reverses course, saying an October 7 massacre documentary will be screened after all. CEO Cameron Bailey apologized and pledged to work with the filmmakers.
One person is dead and two are hospitalized in serious condition as extreme heat grips southern Israel, with temperatures in Eilat soaring to record highs.
The IDF and Shin Bet killed Abdullah Saeed Abd al-Baqin, a Hamas Nukhba deputy commander involved in the October 7 massacre and the abduction of three Israelis.
Israel is enduring a record-breaking heat wave, with overnight temperatures hitting all-time highs and parts of the Jordan Valley and Sea of Galilee expected to approach 50°C today.
About 50 Palestinians attacked a new Jewish farm near Duma in the West Bank, injuring two — including an off-duty IDF soldier who returned fire, wounding one attacker.

The UN is set to blacklist Hamas for conflict-related sexual violence against hostages during the Oct. 7 attack, citing deliberate patterns of abuse, according to an advanced draft of the Secretary-General’s report.
Toronto International Film Festival has pulled a documentary on Hamas’s Oct. 7 massacre, citing copyright issues — reportedly because filmmakers didn’t get Hamas’s permission to use certain videos.
IDF Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir has approved the main plans for an expanded offensive in Gaza, with Shin Bet and senior defense officials present.
IDF said Hamas terrorists used a fake World Central Kitchen vehicle and vests to conceal their movements in Gaza. Five were killed in an airstrike last week.

Italian priest Don Nandino Capovilla from Venice was refused entry to Israel and deported to Greece for “public security” reasons, according to Italian media. He had planned to take part in a Bethlehem pilgrimage with 15 others.
Several haredi men in Jerusalem’s Bukharim neighborhood stole and tore up IDF conscription letters from a postal worker, police said. An investigation is underway.
Local police in The Hague arrested three suspects after vandals hurled red paint and broke the front door of the Israeli embassy, Ynet reported.
Israeli Health Ministry report revealed Gaza hostages face systematic starvation and physical, emotional abuse by Hamas. Released captives show severe malnutrition and untreated injuries.
A fire near Beit Shemesh started from a car and spread to nearby forests, prompting closure of Route 44. Firefighting planes and crews are battling the blaze.

Israel’s hiking trails are under a complete ban as a severe heatwave peaks, with temperatures reaching up to 51°C in the Jordan Valley. Officials warned of health risks like dehydration and heatstroke.
Israeli security forces arrested 42 illegal Palestinian residents from Gaza in Nazareth overnight, police said. Most were found hiding in apartments during a sweep to prevent illegal stay offenses.
Madonna urged Pope Leo XIV to “open humanitarian gates” of Gaza, said she prays for both suffering children and release of Israeli hostages.
France stoped renewing work visas for El Al security staff in Paris amid Gaza war and strained Israel–France relations, Ynet reported.
Greta Thunberg and dozens of vessels plan August 31 Gaza blockade run, accusing Israel of restricting land aid deliveries.
IDF’s 226th Brigade and Unit 504 apprehend weapons dealer in Taranjah, south Syria, seizing arms in overnight raid.
Antisemitic graffiti reading “There is a Holocaust in Gaza” found at Western Wall. Gantz called it a crime against all of Israel and urged police to investigate.
Iranian dissident journalist Niyak Ghorbani assaulted, choked at London march for release of Israeli hostages; attacker threw foul-smelling liquid.
IDF said it killed Hamas member and Al Jazeera journalist Anas Al-Sharif in Gaza, along with five others including another reporter.
Father, son from east Jerusalem arrested for planning Jerusalem bombings; Shin Bet said they built explosives and sought terror group funding.
WATCH: Netanyahu holds press conferences amid global criticism of new Gaza City op.
Hostage families announced Aug. 17 nationwide shutdown to protest Gaza City occupation plan, warning it endangers hostages and soldiers.
Israel braces for record-breaking heatwave this week, with temps forecast up to 49°C near Sea of Galilee and 51°C in the Jordan Valley, IMS warned.
Israeli Security Cabinet approved PM Netanyahu's plan to occupy Gaza City.
IDF uncovered Hamas tunnel and missile launchers beneath Gaza cemetery. The one-kilometer route was mostly dismantled in Daraj Tuffah, the military said.

Families of hostages sailed from Ashkelon Marina to the maritime border with Gaza, demanding their loved ones’ release and an end to the war.

📸: Amir Cohen

El Al’s Paris offices were vandalized with graffiti reading “El Al genocide airline” in what the company called a serious anti-Israel incident. The airline evacuated staff and will operate through a foreign provider at the terminal while French authorities investigate.

The IDF said its forces are continuing operations across Gaza, killing terrorists and destroying infrastructure from both air and ground. Raids are ongoing in Khan Yunis, Jabaliya, Daraj Tofah, and across northern and southern Gaza, with multiple Hamas operatives killed in the past 24 hours.

Hamas is looting humanitarian aid and shooting at Gazan civilians trying to reach it, an IDF commander told Walla. Soldiers have treated wounded civilians on the ground, he said, accusing Hamas of hijacking aid and abusing its own population.

“When we saw the images of Rom and Evyatar—frightened and emaciated—we felt the air leave our lungs,” said Holocaust survivors who met with freed hostages and families of those still in Gaza. The meeting at Hostages Square was a powerful gesture of solidarity and shared pain.

📸: Chen G. Schimmel

“It is possible and necessary to conquer Gaza,” said ex-IDF planning chief Erez Wiener, outlining a 100-day plan to defeat Hamas. His strategy includes seizing Gaza City, controlling humanitarian aid, and relocating civilians to end the war decisively.

Families of hostages expressed panic after new Hamas and PIJ videos showed captives in Gaza. “We have a Holocaust before our eyes,” one relative said, as others warned the government not to endanger them further. A Holocaust survivor in one family broke down after seeing the footage.
The IDF said it struck Hezbollah operative Hossam Qasem Ghorab in Lebanon, who was directing terror cells in Syria planning rocket fire at the Golan.

“Death to the IDF” was graffitied on an American soldier’s family home in St. Louis, where cars were also torched in a suspected antisemitic hate crime. “I am outraged,” said DOJ antisemitism task force chair Leo Terrell. “This has no place in America.”

Austrian heiress Marlene Engelhorn, whose family profited from producing Zyklon B during WWII, said she’ll join the next Gaza flotilla. She inherited over $27M and is now a vocal critic of wealth inequality and Israel’s Gaza policy.

A new exhibition by the Israel Antiquities Authority reveals how archaeologists helped identify missing persons after Oct. 7. “Rising from the Ashes” opens Aug. 6 in Jerusalem, showcasing how ancient skills served a modern tragedy.

Relatives of October 7 victims are suing Meta for over ₪4 billion, accusing Facebook and Instagram of enabling Hamas to livestream the massacre. The footage, they argue, continues to retraumatize families and violate users’ rights.

IDF forces continued operations across Gaza, targeting terrorist infrastructure and killing operatives. In the south, Division 36 struck over 30 Hamas targets. In the north, Givati Brigade destroyed weapons and tunnels, while Division 99 and Fire Brigade 990 hit additional terrorist sites.

“Have we returned to the Holocaust?” asked the mother of hostage Alon Ohel, after Hamas released new videos of captives. Ohel, held for 669 days, is reportedly in critical condition. His family compared the footage to the suffering endured by Alon’s Holocaust-survivor great-grandfather.

Israel will now allow limited goods into Gaza’s private sector, including food and hygiene items, under a new controlled mechanism, COGAT said. The move follows growing pressure over the humanitarian crisis. Payments will be monitored and all items inspected.

Netanyahu has decided on full occupation of Gaza, including areas with hostages, the PMO told the ‘Post’. The message to the IDF chief: if this doesn’t suit you, resign. A broader meeting on Gaza and hostage deals is expected Tuesday.

British pop star Jessie J shared a photo of hostage Evyatar David, calling the images “heartbreaking” and pleading: “Please, God, make the suffering end.” Her post to 14 million followers comes after Hamas released a disturbing video showing David’s emaciated body.

After the release of new hostage videos, the Red Cross and WHO reiterated their demand for the immediate and unconditional release of hostages held in Gaza, warning of the life-threatening and humiliating conditions they face.

Over a dozen House Democrats reportedly urged the Trump administration to recognize a Palestinian state, calling the Gaza crisis a wake-up call for Palestinian self-determination. One lawmaker also plans to introduce a pro-statehood resolution.

Hamas is demanding at least 250 aid trucks enter Gaza daily before it resumes negotiations, a source told the ‘Post’. The message was relayed to mediators recently as part of Hamas’ new stance: no talks without major humanitarian improvements.

Rom Braslavski, held hostage by Palestinian Islamic Jihad, said in a newly released video: “I’ve run out of food, water, I can’t stand.” He appeared frail and in pain. His family approved the video’s release Sunday night.

A Dutch nurse is under investigation after Facebook posts threatened to kill “Zionist” patients via injection. The woman denies writing the messages, claiming her identity was misused. Jewish groups are calling for severe consequences if she’s found responsible.

Fauda star and IDF reservist Idan Amedi slammed a forthcoming petition by Israeli artists against the Gaza war, calling it “detached” and “fake news.” He added: “Go into a tunnel. Fight for one day… You’re worthless—I’ve had enough of you.”
Tens of thousands marched across Sydney’s Harbor Bridge in a pro-Palestinian protest after a court overturned police efforts to block it. A similar march took place in Melbourne.

Hamas’s famine claims questioned after new hostage video. Israel’s Foreign Ministry pointed to the stark contrast between the starved appearance of hostage Evyatar David and the healthy, muscular arm of his Hamas captor—casting doubt on claims that Gazans, including terrorists, are facing famine.

IDF soldiers operated in four locations across southern Syria overnight, arresting multiple suspects involved in weapons smuggling. Numerous weapons were seized, based on prior intel and field investigations, the military said.

Hamas released a new video showing hostage Evyatar David on Friday—his family has not approved sharing the footage. It’s the second such video released in two days, after Palestinian Islamic Jihad posted one of Rom Braslavski.

🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

Iran is using criminal networks in Europe to target Jews and Israeli sites, a US religious freedom report said. The July report also highlighted Iran’s broader persecution of religious minorities and its role in spreading antisemitism abroad.

IDF won’t recognize Roi Wasserstein, a reservist who died by suicide, as a fallen soldier, citing he was off-duty. Roi served over 300 days and spoke often of trauma from his time in the 401st Armored Brigade, Army Radio reported.

COGAT said a viral photo of a Gazan teen said to be starving is misleading—the youth suffers from a genetic condition. The New York Times updated its report to note a similar case involved a child with a pre-existing illness.

The 98th Division has completed its mission in northern Gaza after weeks of fighting in Khan Yunis, Shejaiya and Zeitoun. Troops dismantled 1,500 terror sites and killed dozens of Hamas operatives, including some involved in the October 7 massacre.
Hasbara activist Yoseph Haddad was detained after accidentally firing a bullet during an argument between drivers in Jaffa. Police say two people were detained; no injuries were reported.
A 15-year-old was shot in the back after a reservist fired at stone-throwers near the evacuated Tzur Harel outpost. He is conscious; military and police are investigating.
In an open letter, 31 Israeli figures urged the world to impose “crippling sanctions” to force a ceasefire in Gaza. The signatories accused Israel of starving Gazans and planning mass displacement.

The Israeli Air Force will get its first two KC-46A aerial refuelers by 2026, boosting its ability to strike distant targets. The $930M deal includes up to 8 aircraft, replacing Israel’s aging 707 fleet.

The Gaza Humanitarian Foundation rejected claims by former contractor Lt.-Col. Anthony Aguilar, who told the BBC he saw unarmed civilians shot at aid sites. GHF called him a “disgruntled contractor” and said an internal probe found his testimony false.

The NYT amended an article on Gaza starvation to note that a child it featured had a preexisting medical condition — a detail initially omitted. The change followed a request from Israel’s Consulate in New York, which provided the child’s medical history.
🚨 Sirens sound in central Israel.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

IDF: We’ll keep striking Hezbollah until it disarms — even if it means war. Despite the November ceasefire, the IDF has carried out over 500 strikes on Hezbollah and says it will continue until the group is disarmed. Hezbollah’s rocket and launcher stockpiles have reportedly dropped by up to 80%.

Israeli actor Alon Aboutboul, 60, found dead on Tel Aviv beach. One of Israel’s most acclaimed actors, Aboutboul collapsed on HaBonim Beach Tuesday morning. CPR efforts failed to revive him. The cause of death is still unknown. He is survived by his wife and four children.
Israel's best-selling English-language newspaper is seeking a talented individual to join our passionate team! Could that be you or someone you know?

Yemen’s Houthis released a video of 10 crew members from the Greek ship ETERNITY C, which was sunk in the Red Sea earlier this month. The crew, presumed to be held by the group, said the Houthis “saved” them while they were floating and have since provided shelter and safety.

The IDF released new footage showing Hamas terrorists looting humanitarian aid in Gaza. “Even when aid is delivered into Gaza – Hamas loots it for its own use, blatantly disregarding the needs of the population,” the IDF said, countering Hamas’s claims that the men in the video were security personnel.

An Israeli official told The Jerusalem Post that the military will consider expanding its operation in Gaza if ongoing negotiations continue to stall. The official stated, “If there’s no advance in the talks, we will have no choice but to expand the military operation."

Israeli human rights group B’Tselem accused the government of carrying out a “coordinated action to intentionally destroy Palestinian society in Gaza” in a new report published Monday. The group said the campaign amounts to a “developing genocidal regime,” citing testimonies, data, and official statements.

13 activists from the Gaza flotilla ‘Handala’ face deportation hearings today after trying to break Israel’s maritime blockade. The boat, which left Sicily with humanitarian supplies, is the second such attempt in weeks, following the interception of the Madleen.

The remains of an IDF reservist were discovered in his home in southern Israel, the military said.
He had previously served in the Military Rabbinate, identifying fallen soldiers.

Former hostage Ofer Kalderon was honored at the final stage of the Tour de France in Paris, invited by Israel’s Premier Tech cycling team. A cycling fan, he said the moment was emotional — but bittersweet, as hostages remain in Gaza and soldiers continue to fall.

“We are not blocking aid,” IDF Spokesperson BG Effie Defrin said from Kerem Shalom. Israel approved 250+ aid trucks this week and continues airdrops and daily humanitarian pauses. “Israel supports aid for civilians, not for Hamas.”
IDF troops from the 646th Reservist Brigade dismantled a Hamas tunnel about 500 meters long in Beit Hanoun, as part of a months-long operation.
IDF announced localized Gaza ceasefires to expand food aid 'until further notice'.
M.-Sgt. Betzalel Yehoshua Mosbacher succumbed to wounds sustained in battle in southern Gaza.

Two soldiers from the Golani Brigade were killed in southern Gaza, the IDF said. Captain Amir Saad and Sergeant Inon Nuriel Vana, both 22, served in the brigade’s reconnaissance unit and fell during operational activity.
🚨 Sirens sound in Dead Sea region.
🚨 IDF warns of incoming missiles from Yemen. Israelis advised to stay near shelters ahead of expected sirens.

A new US report claims no proof of Hamas stealing humanitarian aid, but multiple testimonies suggest otherwise. As hunger deepens in Gaza, questions grow about the true fate of aid meant for civilians.

With over 2,500 tons of food stuck at the border, the Gaza Humanitarian Foundation has offered to deliver UN aid into Gaza for free. The offer comes as hunger deepens and the UN faces criticism for failing to distribute the aid piling up at Kerem Shalom Crossing.

72 Knesset members from across party lines signed a statement calling Taiwan’s exclusion from global forums “unjustified.” The move reflects gratitude for Taiwan’s support since Oct. 7 and urges its inclusion in organizations like the WHO and ICAO.

A rocket launched by terrorists from Khan Yunis landed just 250 meters from an aid distribution site in Rafah, the IDF said. Despite the attack, the site opened Thursday and food was delivered to families.
Seven people were wounded after a car rammed into pedestrians near Kfar Yona on Thursday. Magen David Adom is treating the injured, and police are investigating it as a possible terror attack.

Members of the haredi community blocked the entrance to Jerusalem on Wednesday while protesting against the haredi draft law.

Hezbollah refuses to give up its weapons—even if Israel pulls out of southern Lebanon, reports said. Despite a ceasefire, Hezbollah has rejected any long-term deal involving disarmament. Lebanese officials are frustrated, but the group remains defiant, signaling it’s ready to confront critics at home.
A woman in her 70s was arrested in central Israel for plotting to assassinate PM Netanyahu. She is a known anti-govt protester who sought weapons and inquired about security details.

In January, Israel withdrew from deeper areas like Gaza City and Khan Yunis to a buffer zone. The new deal could restore that same positioning.

Iranian officials suspect Israel is behind a recent wave of fires and explosions, The New York Times reported. Though authorities blame infrastructure issues publicly, IRGC sources told the NYT they view it as Israeli sabotage—but avoid open accusations to sidestep pressure to retaliate.

Israeli tanks pushed into Deir al-Balah on Monday as part of a deeper incursion into Gaza. The area is crowded with displaced Palestinians, many of whom fled again after the IDF issued evacuation orders. Israel said it’s targeting Hamas strongholds and possibly searching for hostages.

Two IDF soldiers visiting Belgium for the Tomorrowland festival were briefly detained and questioned over alleged war crimes, according to the Hind Rajab Foundation, an anti-Israel legal group. They were later released after the investigation.

The IDF struck Houthi targets at Yemen’s Hodeidah port on Monday in response to recent ballistic missile attacks on Israel. It’s the first such strike since July 7, which had little effect on halting Houthi launches. Defense Minister Israel Katz warned: “The rule for Yemen is the same as the rule for Tehran.”

Footage shared by the IDF shows Gazans cheering while receiving humanitarian aid, with soldiers watching from a distance and ordered not to shoot. “Not a single bullet was fired,” said spokesperson Avichay Adraee, adding that civilians welcomed the soldiers “as if they saw in them life after darkness.”
The IDF and Shin Bet struck the hideout of top terrorist commander Hussein Fiad, known as Abu Hamza, in Beit Hanun. They are now waiting for confirmation that he was killed in the targeted operation.

Iran confirmed it has replaced air defenses damaged in last month’s conflict with Israel. During the exchange, Israel dominated Iranian airspace, striking key systems as Iran launched waves of missiles and drones.

Hostage families are warning the IDF against launching operations in Deir al-Balah, fearing for their loved ones’ safety. “Can anyone guarantee this won’t cost us their lives?” the Hostages Forum asked after an evacuation was announced for the area.

IDF combat engineers from the 401st Brigade dismantled a 2.7 km tunnel network beneath Jabalya, part of the military’s expanding operations in Gaza. Dozens of terrorists have been killed and weapons seized, though the cost is high—three soldiers were killed last week inside their tank.
IDF issued evacuation warnings for residents in Deir al-Balah in area it has not operated in before.

Body of man missing for two years found in Rishon Lezion apartment.

IDF said its 36th Division completed the opening of the “Magen Oz” Corridor in Khan Yunis. Golani and 188th Armored Brigades killed dozens of terrorists and uncovered tunnels and weapons along the 15-km route.
US Ambassador Mike Huckabee showed up at Netanyahu’s trial, drawing parallels to Trump’s “witch trial” in the US. “I’m an observer,” he said, as part of the hearing moved behind closed doors.

20 Palestinians killed in deadly stampede at Gaza aid site. GHF said 19 were trampled, 1 stabbed — blaming crowd agitators, believed to be Hamas. The incident marked the first fatal tragedy on GHF grounds.

Flash floods swamp NYC and New Jersey as summer storms paralyze streets and subways. Passengers were trapped in subway cars as water poured in.

United Torah Judaism announced it will leave the government coalition. Shas, the Sephardic haredi party, has not yet said if it will follow.
An IDF soldier who served in Gaza during the Israel-Hamas war took his own life at a base in the Golan Heights.

Three IDF soldiers from the 401st Armored Brigade were killed and one wounded in a tank explosion in northern Gaza. The fallen: Staff-Sgt. Shoham Menahem, Sgt. Shlomo Yakir Shrem, and Sgt. Yuliy Faktor.

The IDF and Shin Bet killed Muhammad Nasr Ali Quneita, a Hamas terrorist who infiltrated Israel on October 7 and held Israeli hostage Emily Damari in his home. He was killed in a targeted strike in Gaza City on June 19.

The IDF struck Syrian tanks that advanced toward Sweida, a Druze city near Israel’s border, amid ongoing sectarian violence. Over 30 people were killed and 100 injured in clashes earlier Monday, according to reports.

An IDF probe found that the military failed to defend Ofakim on October 7, when 15 Hamas terrorists infiltrated the city, killing 33 people—25 of them civilians. Security forces and armed locals eventually killed all the attackers.

Degel HaTorah is expected to exit the coalition in 24 hours if a draft law is not presented, Channel 12 reports. Shas may also withdraw from the coalition before the end of the session next week if the party sees no draft law has advanced to a vote.

IDF and ISA killed top Hamas and Islamic Jihad terrorists involved in rebuilding military capabilities. In the past two weeks, precision strikes killed senior figures in weapons production, military intelligence, and terror financing.
More than 30 killed in sectarian clashes in Syria's Sweida, interior ministry said.
Leah Mosquera, Filipino caregiver, succumbed to wounds after Iranian missile attack in Rehovot.
A kosher restaurant in Athens was vandalized just 12 minutes after Shabbat ended. Hooligans threw flyers and sprayed anti-Israel graffiti on the walls, the owner told ‘Post’.
Foreign airlines are returning to Israel faster than expected: Over 100 new flights are set for August, including 56 to NYC and 41 to Europe. United Airlines, Wizz Air, and Aegean lead the comeback.
Israel launched its Dror-1 satellite with SpaceX, reaching 36,000 km above Earth. Developed by IAI, the mission marked a leap in Israeli space tech.
Netanyahu is expected to meet with a small group of ministers Sunday evening to discuss the ongoing hostage-ceasefire negotiations.

Palestinian officials claim Gaza hostage-ceasefire talks on verge of collapse - report
Trump not opposed to another Israeli attack on Islamic Republic if nuclear program continues - WSJ
The IDF confirmed it killed two key Palestinian Islamic Jihad terrorists—one of whom infiltrated Israel on October 7. Forces also dismantled a 1-km tunnel and struck a terror cell in Khan Yunis.
Israel’s ex-defense minister sends unprecedented letter to Iran’s supreme leader
IDF soldier Captain Reei Biran killed in the southern Gaza Strip.
'Another step toward freedom': Romi Gonen released after five months in Sheba Hospital.
IDF destroys 500-meter Hamas tunnel in Khan Yunis. Golani and Yahalom troops also dismantled over 130 terror sites and killed dozens of terrorists across Gaza this week.
Suspected terror attack at Gush Etzion junction kills 20-year-old Israeli. Several others injured in the incident, according to police and emergency services.
IDF forces demolished illegal structures in the West Bank village of Shweika, including a greenhouse and a building used to fire at Israeli communities.

Six crew members from the Greek ship sunk in the Red Sea are believed to be held by the Houthis, according to maritime security sources. The Houthis claimed they had “rescued” several crew but offered no details.

An IDF soldier was found dead at a base in southern Israel, the military confirmed. The death is not related to Gaza operations but to an internal disciplinary issue. His name has not yet been released, but the family has been notified.
Staff-Sergeant-Major Abraham Azulay killed in Khan Yunis during attempted abduction.

An IDF soldier was killed while fighting off an attempted kidnapping by terrorists in Khan Yunis, the military said. His name has not yet been cleared for publication, but his family has been notified.

The Givati Brigade joined the 99th Division to encircle Beit Hanoun in northern Gaza, the IDF said. Troops are targeting Hamas terrorists and dismantling infrastructure under the command of the 162nd Division.

The Trump administration will subpoena Harvard over alleged antisemitism and harassment by foreign students. It also warned the school’s accreditor of possible federal violations—putting its accreditation and student aid eligibility at risk.
Three protesters were charged with assault and criminal damage after pro-Palestinian activists stormed Miznon, an Israeli restaurant in Melbourne.
IDF troops dismantled Hezbollah terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon, locating weapons depots, firing positions, and an underground storage site.
The IDF struck over 100 terror targets in Gaza, hitting tunnels, anti-tank launch posts, weapons depots, and booby-trapped buildings.
A Greek-operated ship sank off Yemen after a deadly Houthi attack. Two crew members were killed, and rescue efforts are underway, with at least five survivors pulled from the water so far.

Israel struck and killed Hussein Ali Muzhir, Hezbollah’s firepower chief in the Zahrani sector. The IDF said he played a key role in attacks on Israeli forces and efforts to build artillery positions in southern Lebanon.

Israel killed senior Hamas commander Mehran Ba‘jur in an airstrike near Tripoli. The IDF said he led rocket attacks on northern cities and spent years building Hamas’s military infrastructure in Lebanon.

Former hostage Keith Siegel told the Knesset he spent 484 days in Gaza and said the remaining 50 hostages have now endured 641. “It’s hard to describe their suffering,” he said, recalling his own experience and urging action for those still held.

Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa reportedly met Israeli National Security Adviser Tzachi Hanegbi in Abu Dhabi, according to a Syrian outlet. Sources claimed this wasn’t the first such meeting. Talks on a potential peace agreement are said to be ongoing.

Former hostage Karina Ariev completed her IDF service five months after being freed from Hamas captivity. In an emotional post, she honored her fallen comrades, saying, “I wish everyone was here…” Ariev was kidnapped from the Nahal Oz base on October 7.

A new report by the Dinah Project concluded that Hamas used sexual violence as a tactical weapon of war on October 7. The authors, a team of legal and gender experts, said this has serious implications under international law and mirrors patterns seen in conflicts like Iraq and Nigeria.

A senior Israeli official told ‘Post’ a Gaza hostage deal could be reached “within days.” Despite Hamas rejecting the latest Qatari proposal, the gaps are “small” and talks are still moving forward.

A US-backed aid group proposed building “Humanitarian Transit Areas” in or outside Gaza to temporarily house Palestinians and reduce Hamas’s influence. The $2B plan was submitted to the Trump administration and described the camps as “voluntary” sites for deradicalization and relocation.

Israel struck three Houthi-controlled ports in Yemen early Monday—Hodeidah, Ras Issa, and Al-Salif—along with a ship and a power station used for terror purposes, the IDF said. Defense Minister Katz called it part of efforts to dismantle Houthi terror infrastructure.

Ramzi Saleh, a key Hamas naval official, and two mortar operatives were killed in an Israeli airstrike in Gaza City, the IDF and Shin Bet said Sunday. Troops from the Nahal Brigade also operated in the Daraj Tuffah area.
